title: ChatGPT share links abused to host fake outage pages to deliver malware
summary: Threat actors are abusing ChatGPT's content-sharing feature to display fake OpenAI outage pages that direct users to download malware disguised as the ChatGPT desktop application. [...]

title: California AG sues 23andMe over 2023 breach exposing health data
summary: California Attorney General Rob Bonta filed a lawsuit against 23andMe, now Chrome Holding Co., over the company's failure to protect sensitive customer genetic and personal information. [...]

title: From $5 Attacks to Botnet-Powered Platforms: Inside the DDoS-as-a- Service Market
summary: DDoS attacks are increasingly being sold like subscription services, complete with pricing tiers, support, and reseller programs. Flare explores how the DDoS-as-a-Service market has evolved from scattered tools into polished attack platforms. [...]

title: Dutch govt disrupts malware botnet with 17 million infected devices
summary: Dutch authorities have taken offline a massive botnet of 17 million devices and seized more than 200 servers at a local provider that supported the operation. [...]

title: Google Chrome adds session cookie theft protection for all users
summary: Google says the Chrome Device Bound Session Credentials (DBSC) security feature is now generally available and is rolling out to all users to prevent account takeovers. [...]

title: Man sent to prison for selling data of 7 millions elderly Americans
summary: A North Carolina man was sentenced to more than 10 years in prison for selling the personal information of over 7 million elderly Americans to Jamaican scammers. [...]

title: US charges Google security engineer with Polymarket insider trading
summary: A Google security engineer was charged with insider trading after winning $1.2 million using confidential company data to place bets on the cryptocurrency-based Polymarket decentralized prediction market. [...]

title: Charter Communications data breach affects 4.9 million accounts
summary: The ShinyHunters extortion gang stole personal information from 4.9 million accounts after hacking the U.S. telecom giant Charter Communications in early April, according to data breach notification service Have I Been Pwned. [...]

title: Anthropic confirms Claude Mythos-class models will roll out to the public
summary: Anthropic has confirmed that it plans to bring Mythos-class models to the general public after delaying the rollout due to security risks to public and private software. [...]

title: GreyVibe hackers use ChatGPT, Gemini to power cyberattacks
summary: A likely Russian threat cluster tracked as GreyVibe has been targeting Ukrainian entities with AI-generated lures and a rich set of custom malware tools. [...]

title: BTMOB Android malware service generates custom phishing payloads
summary: An Android remote access trojan named BTMOB is offered to cybercriminals with a builder interface for generating malware payloads tailored to phishing lures. [...]

title: FBI warns of fake FIFA websites running World Cup fraud schemes
summary: The FBI is warning of fake websites impersonating FIFA ahead of the 2026 World Cup, to steal personal and financial information, sell fake tickets and hospitality packages, and push other fraud related to the event. [...]

title: Hackers exploit FortiClient EMS flaw to push infostealer malware
summary: Hackers are exploiting an authentication bypass vulnerability (CVE-2026-35616) in FortiClient Enterprise Management Server (EMS) to deliver an undocumented credential stealer called EKZ. [...]

title: New Gogs zero-day flaw lets hackers get remote code execution
summary: An unpatched zero-day vulnerability in the Gogs self-hosted Git service can allow attackers to gain remote code execution (RCE) on Internet-facing instances. [...]

title: How SIEM helps MSPs reduce noise and stop threats faster
summary: MSPs don't lack security data. They struggle to separate real threats from alert noise. Kaseya explains how SIEM helps MSPs improve visibility, reduce fatigue, and respond faster. [...]
